premise: After my husband died, his sister convinced me to transfer my $280,000 paid-off house into a trust she would manage because she had terminal cancer—then she miraculously recovered, sold the house, and kept the cash. I proved the trust termination was a forgery, won a fraud judgment, reclaimed the property, and now rent it out while she lives in a studio; I send her monthly tax bills to her new address.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Sister-In-Law Faked Terminal Cancer, Stole My $280K Home. So I Sent Her Tax B
